# AGENTS

Shared Agent Pack is the source-backed command, agent, skill, and MCP system for this repository.

## Source of truth

- Edit shared definitions in `agent-pack/core/`.
- Edit MCP registrations in `agent-pack/mcp/registry.json`.
- Regenerate host outputs with `node scripts/build-agent-pack.mjs`.
- Do not hand-edit generated files in `.claude/`, `AGENTS.md`, or `agent-pack/dist/`.

## Commands

- `build-feature`: Implement a scoped feature in the current repository, validate the result, and summarize the change set. Workflow: planner -> coder -> reviewer. Skills: repo-conventions.
- `review-diff`: Review a diff or pending change set for bugs, regressions, risky assumptions, and missing tests. Workflow: reviewer. Skills: repo-conventions.

## Subagents

- `coder`: Implementation specialist. Mission: Make the smallest high-confidence code change that satisfies the request, then verify it. Deliver: Code changes with a short rationale. Validation results and known follow-ups.
- `planner`: Scoping and sequencing specialist. Mission: Clarify the task, break it into minimal steps, and decide whether handoff to implementation or review is appropriate. Deliver: A short plan with key assumptions. A handoff note for the next agent.
- `reviewer`: Risk-focused reviewer. Mission: Find correctness issues, behavior regressions, missing tests, and hidden assumptions before the change ships. Deliver: Ordered findings with severity and supporting context. Residual risks or testing gaps if no findings are discovered.

## Skills

- `find-skills`: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. Path: `agent-pack/core/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md`.
- `frontend-design`: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics. Path: `agent-pack/core/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md`.
- `repo-conventions`: Apply the shared conventions for this repository's cross-host agent pack. Use when adding or updating commands, subagents, skills, MCP registry entries, generator scripts, or host adapters for Claude Code and Codex. Path: `agent-pack/core/skills/repo-conventions/SKILL.md`.
- `vercel-react-best-practices`: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements. Path: `agent-pack/core/skills/vercel-react-best-practices/SKILL.md`.
- `web-design-guidelines`: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices". Path: `agent-pack/core/skills/web-design-guidelines/SKILL.md`.

## MCP registry

- `github`: Repository, pull request, issue, and diff context for implementation and review workflows. Command: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github`.
- `notion`: Optional product-document context for specs, PRDs, and team notes. Command: `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-notion`.

## Operating rules

- Load `repo-conventions` before changing the agent pack.
- Keep commands orchestration-only and push reusable knowledge into skills.
- Prefer the smallest safe change and report validation status clearly.
